questions.question: What did the 2026 structural equation modeling study (n=842) find about technology use and wellbeing?

options.text: Total screen time was the strongest predictor of mental health outcomes
Intentional, goal-directed use predicted better wellbeing — independent of total screen time
Passive social media use always produces worse outcomes than active use
All social media use increases anxiety and depression equally

questions.explanation: Intentional, goal-directed technology use predicted better attentional control (β=0.42) and psychological wellbeing (β=0.37), while passive, habitual use showed negative associations. Quality of use matters more than quantity.

questions.question: Which of the following claims has STRONG evidence in the current research base?

options.text: Social media is the primary cause of the adolescent mental health crisis
Nighttime screen use suppresses melatonin and delays circadian rhythm via blue light
Digital detox produces durable mental health improvement
Screen addiction is a formal clinical diagnosis in the DSM-5

questions.explanation: The mechanism of blue light → ipRGCs → suprachiasmatic nucleus → melatonin suppression is STRONG evidence. The other claims are either overstated (social media as primary cause), have mixed evidence (digital detox), or are not supported (screen addiction is not a DSM-5 diagnosis).

questions.question: According to the three-tier framework, what tier does this course operate in?

options.text: Tier 3: Treatment-Level Claims — this course treats mental health conditions
Tiers 1 and 2: Wellness Education and Adjunctive Self-Management
Only Tier 1: General wellness tips with no clinical relevance
All three tiers — this course can replace therapy

questions.explanation: This course teaches evidence-based wellness strategies (Tier 1) and skills that support professional care (Tier 2) — but does not replace professional mental health assessment or treatment (Tier 3).

questions.question: Why is 'screen time' considered a poor predictor of mental health outcomes?

options.text: Because people underreport their actual screen time
Because many heavy users show no mental health problems — it's the negative consequences (distress, interference, inability to stop) that predict outcomes
Because all screens are equally harmful regardless of use
Because screen time measurement is impossible

questions.explanation: The Karolinska study (n=2,265) found that heavy involvement alone didn't predict problems — it was the experience of harm and functional impairment that mattered. Quality of use > quantity of use.

questions.question: Think about your own technology use. What's one area where you currently use technology *habitually or passively* rather than *intentionally and goal-directed*? How does it make you feel afterward?

title: The Science of Digital Wellness - Quiz
